slides.title: [Client name] concise review
slides.subtitle: [Purpose, scope, decision focus, and timing for the working diligence pack]

slides.title: [One clear diligence claim]
slides.strapline: [State why the issue matters now, what evidence supports it, and what decision leadership should be ready to take.]
body: [Summarize the current state with one concrete operating or financial fact that gives the audience immediate context for the diligence issue.]
[Explain the most material gap with supporting evidence from management, data, or process observation so the slide does real analytical work.]
[State the practical implication for timing, ownership, or transaction value creation rather than leaving the point at a descriptive level.]
[Name the action leadership should sponsor next and connect it to the expected benefit, mitigation, or decision milestone.]
[Call out the dependency, execution risk, or unanswered question that still needs follow-up work before the conclusion is fully closed.]

slides.title: [Current vs target]
slides.strapline: [Use for a balanced side-by-side comparison that still reads like consulting commentary instead of short presentation bullets.]
leftBody: [Describe the current state with one grounded fact and explain the operational consequence if the issue remains unresolved.]
[Explain the root cause or control gap in practical terms so the audience understands why the current model is underperforming.]
[Name the most visible business consequence in revenue, margin, timing, or execution confidence.]
rightBody: [Describe the target state and explain how it resolves the current issue with a clearer operating model, control, or decision path.]
[State the first action, responsible owner, and the near-term milestone that proves the change is underway.]
[Connect the target state to a measurable benefit, risk reduction, or transaction-readiness outcome.]

slides.title: [Structured evidence table]
slides.strapline: [Use for a compact evidence table with real read-through instead of a high row count.]

rows: Explain why the gap matters and what management must do next.
Link the shortfall to execution discipline, ownership, or timing.
State the implication for the diligence conclusion or action plan.
Keep the read-through concise so the layout stays on one page.

insights: [Call out the most material gap and explain why it matters for performance, execution certainty, transaction readiness, and the broader diligence conclusion.]
[State the operational implication in terms of timing, ownership, and the control step management must strengthen next to close the gap credibly.]
[Name the priority action leadership should sponsor and the evidence needed to close the remaining diligence question with confidence.]
